{
  "title": "[Process Doc Update]: README Created for OctoAcme Project Management Documentation Hub",
  "labels": ["documentation", "process improvement"],
  "body": "## Which process document do you want to update?\n\n**<documento nuevo>** - This is a new documentation artifact\n\n## Summary of New Content\n\nCreated a comprehensive README.md file for the `docs/` directory that serves as a centralized hub for all OctoAcme Project Management Documentation. The README:\n\n- ✅ Provides an overview of OctoAcme's project management approach and core principles\n- ✅ Includes a brief summary of all lifecycle stages (Initiation, Planning, Execution, Release, Retrospective)\n- ✅ Contains organized links to all process documentation:\n  - Project Management Overview\n  - Project Initiation Guide\n  - Project Planning\n  - Execution & Tracking\n  - Risk Management & Communication\n  - Release & Deployment Guide\n  - Retrospective & Continuous Improvement\n  - Roles & Personas\n- ✅ Defines core roles and key artifacts used across projects\n- ✅ Provides a \"Getting Started\" guide directing users to appropriate documents based on their context\n- ✅ Includes guidance on how to contribute updates to the documentation\n\n## Why is this update needed?\n\nPreviously, the process documentation existed as separate markdown files without a central hub or navigation guide. This created friction for:\n\n- **New team members** who need to quickly understand OctoAcme's approach but don't know where to start\n- **Project team members** who need to find specific guidance for their current project phase\n- **Knowledge discovery** - there was no single source of truth for browsing all available process documentation\n- **Onboarding** - the lack of an index made it harder to introduce people to structured project management practices\n\nThe README solves these problems by:\n- Accelerating onboarding and reducing time to productivity\n- Serving as a table of contents for the process documentation\n- Reinforcing OctoAcme's core principles and consistent approach\n- Making the documentation more discoverable and usable\n\n## Content Included\n\n### 1. **Introduction & Core Principles**\n- Welcome header explaining the purpose of the documentation hub\n- OctoAcme's five core principles (Customer-first, Iterative delivery, Clear ownership, Data-informed decisions, Psychological safety)\n\n### 2. **Project Lifecycle Stages**\n- Brief descriptions of each phase (Initiation, Planning, Execution, Release, Close & Retrospective)\n- Clear links to detailed documentation for each stage\n\n### 3. **Key Documentation Links**\n- Organized list with clear titles, descriptions, and direct markdown links to each process document:\n  - 📋 Project Management Overview\n  - 🚀 Project Initiation Guide\n  - 📐 Project Planning\n  - ⚙️ Execution & Tracking\n  - 📊 Risk Management & Communication\n  - 🎯 Release & Deployment Guide\n  - 🔄 Retrospective & Continuous Improvement\n  - 👥 Roles & Personas\n\n### 4. **Core Roles Quick Reference**\n- PM (Project Manager)\n- PdM (Product Manager)\n- Developers\n- QA/Testing\n- Stakeholders\n\n### 5. **Key Artifacts Summary**\n- List of main deliverables used across projects\n\n### 6. **Communication Cadence**\n- Quick reference for meeting structure and frequency\n\n### 7. **Getting Started Guide**\n- Decision tree helping users find the right document based on their situation:\n  - New to OctoAcme? → Project Management Overview\n  - Starting a new project? → Project Initiation Guide\n  - In execution? → Execution & Tracking\n  - Need to escalate? → Risk Management & Communication\n  - Planning a release? → Release & Deployment Guide\n\n### 8. **Contributing Guidelines**\n- How to propose updates to process docs using the issue template\n\n### 9. **Contact & Feedback Section**\n- Where to ask questions or propose improvements\n\n### 10. **Metadata**\n- Last-updated timestamp\n- Note that these are living documents that evolve with team practices\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- [x] Content aligns with existing process docs\n- [x] Update improves clarity or closes a documented gap\n- [x] Proposed content has been reviewed with stakeholders (if needed)\n\n## File Location\n\n**Created at:** `docs/README.md`\n\n## Additional Notes\n\n- All links are relative markdown links pointing to the other documentation files\n- Uses clear formatting (headings, bullet points, emojis) for easy scanning\n- Includes a last-updated timestamp at the bottom\n- Designed as a \"living document\" that evolves with team practices"
}
